text: Hezbollah Warns US-Iran Ceasefire Could Collapse Over Lebanon Hezbollah Spokesperson Ibrahim Moussawi warned U.S.-Iran ceasefire could collapse if Lebanon front is excluded, as Israel continues strikes & regional tensions rise with new attacks across Gulf states. READ MORE

BREAKING: Trump: The United States will work closely with Iran, which we have determined has gone through what will be a very productive Regime Change! There will be no enrichment of Uranium, and the United States will, working with Iran, dig up and remove all of the deeply buried (B-2 Bombers) Nuclear “Dust.” It is now, and has been, under very exacting Satellite Surveillance (Space Force!). Nothing has been touched from the date of attack. We are, and will be, talking Tariff and Sanctions relief with Iran. Many of the 15 points have already been been agreed to.

After the 2024 pager attack on Hezbollah, Hungary’s foreign minister Peter Szijjarto privately offered intelligence-sharing help to Iran. Source: WaPo

European governments are growing uneasy with Mark Rutte because he has been publicly supportive of Trump’s aggressive moves, including the Iran strikes, even when many European leaders strongly disagreed. His strategy of flattering Trump and echoing his rhetoric has given him direct access and some influence, but it’s now being seen as too deferential and potentially undermining Europe’s own position. Source: Bloomberg

Status-6 (War & Military News) A video showing fire on the territory of the Lavan refinery situated on Iran's Lavan Island in the Persian Gulf. The National Iranian ​Oil Refining ​and Distribution Company said that the fire was caused by an "enemy attack," without specifying who was responsible for it. Iran’s state broadcaster later reported that Iranian forces launched series of strikes against the United Arab Emirates and Kuwait as a response to the attack on the Lavan refinery. tweet

OSINTdefender According to the United Arab Emirates (UAE) Ministry of Defense, on April 8th, the UAE’s air defenses engaged 17 Iranian ballistic missiles and 35 Iranian drones. 3 people sustained minor injuries in the strikes. The total tally of Iranian strikes on the UAE is now 2,256 drones, 26 cruise missiles, and 537 ballistic missiles. The UAE has borne the brunt of Iranian missile and drone strikes on its Gulf neighbors. tweet

OSINTdefender Iranian state news outlets are blaming the U.S. and Israel for recent strikes on the oil refinery on Lavan Island. No word from the Israel Defense Force or CENTCOM at the time of writing https://twitter.com/sentdefender/status/2041833927237685470/photo/1 tweet

Visioner 🇮🇷 🇦🇪 🇰🇼 It is likely that Arab countries struck Iran to prevent it from developing a sense of impunity in the region. Notably, the strike targeted Sirri Island, where, similar to Kharg Island, key oil facilities are located. This appears to be a signal that if Iran continues its attacks on Arab states, they may—unlike the Americans—target Kharg Island itself, which handles around 90% of Iran’s oil exports. 🇮🇷 Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) reported on last night's “100th wave” of Operation True Promise 4, calling it a direct response to recent attacks on Iranian infrastructure. It said it does not trust Western promises and warned that any further aggression will be met with stronger retaliation. According to the IRGC, more than 25 strategic targets were hit across the region—from U.S. positions in the Middle East to key sites inside Israel—using ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, and drones. Targets reportedly included major energy infrastructure linked to U.S. companies such as Chevron and ExxonMobil across Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the UAE, Bahrain, and Kuwait, including oil refineries, petrochemical plants, and pipeline networks bypassing the Strait of Hormuz. Inside Israel, the IRGC claims strikes on the Haifa oil refinery, Ben Gurion Airport, intelligence and surveillance centers, government command facilities, and advanced technology sites in Tel Aviv and Be’er Sheva. The statement also claims attacks on U.S. military assets, including bases in Jordan and Kuwait, as well as naval forces operating in regional waters. The IRGC said the operation stretched from the Mediterranean to the eastern Arabian Peninsula, delivering heavy blows to enemy infrastructure, and warned that its forces remain ready to escalate further if provoked again. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇮🇷 ⚡️ IRGC: The enemy has always deceived us, and we have no trust in its promises. We will respond to any aggression at a higher level. Last night, with divine grace and the support of the Iranian nation, the IRGC and Basij forces carried out the 100th wave of Operation True Promise 4 (code name: Ya Fatimah al-Zahra). They struck more than 25 strategic targets, including 13 energy complexes and oil lines linked to America and the Zionist regime, plus 10 military, security, and logistical targets. The Aerospace Force hit enemy targets from the Mediterranean shores to eastern Hijaz in honor of the martyred Leader Imam Khamenei. The IRGC remains fully prepared under the command of Ayatollah Sayyed Mojtaba Khamenei , hands on the trigger, ready for an even greater response if the enemy miscalculates again. The enemy always lies - we trust none of its promises and will answer any attack with greater force.

🇷🇺 👍 🇮🇷 🤝 🇺🇸 / 🇷🇺 - 🇭🇺 : Dimitry Peskov: The Kremlin has welcomed the news of a ceasefire between the US and Iran and approves of the decision to avoid further armed escalation. 💬 The Kremlin hopes that direct contacts between the US and Iran will take place in the coming days. He also touched on the internet and VPN related issues inside Russian Federation and the upcoming Hungarian parliamentary elections and foreign meddling in them. Key points: ➡️ The Kremlin has always stressed the need to move the escalation in the Middle East onto a diplomatic track as quickly as possible; ➡️ Russia expects the US and Iran to defend their interests at the negotiating table; ➡️ The Kremlin hopes that American negotiators will soon have more time to focus on resolving the Ukraine conflict; ➡️ Russia greatly values Trump’s peacekeeping efforts and those of the US negotiating team on the Ukraine track; ➡️ The Ukraine and Iran settlement processes are only indirectly linked; ➡️ Russian agencies are working to restore normal internet operations; ➡️ Conclusions about internet restrictions and VPN usage in Russia should be left to experts; ➡️ Nothing in the published transcript of the Putin–Orbán call could harm Russian-Hungarian relations; ➡️ The published transcript can be seen as material supporting the Hungarian Prime Minister; ➡️ The transcript shows Orbán as a pragmatic politician protecting his country’s interests; ➡️ Many forces in the EU and Brussels would rather not see Orbán win another election and are backing his opponents. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🟡 Statement issued by Hezbollah: To the noblest of people... To the people of the patient, steadfast, and sacrificing resistance... To the people of the steadfast South, the towering Bekaa, and the proud Dahiyeh... We stand today at the threshold of a great historic victory, which will be achieved thanks to the sacrifices of the mujahideen, the blood of the martyrs, and your unparalleled steadfastness and patience... We call upon you in these decisive moments to exercise even more patience, steadfastness, and waiting, and not to head to the villages, towns, and targeted areas in the South, the Bekaa, and the Southern Suburb of Beirut until the official final ceasefire declaration in Lebanon is issued. This is because the treacherous and barbaric enemy, which is seeking to escape from the image of its defeat, may resort to treacherous attempts to create an illusionary scene suggesting it has achieved something it could not attain on the ground. God willing, you will soon return to your villages and homes with heads held high, proud, dignified, victorious — just as you have always been.

🇮🇷 ⚔️ 🇦🇪 An oil refinery on Iran’s Lavan Island was attacked this morning, the National Iranian Oil Refining and Distribution Company confirmed. No casualties have been reported. The attack reportedly originated from the UAE, and it appears that Iran has already retaliated. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
Strikes hit an Iranian oil refinery on Lavan Island today. Iran is now retaliating with strikes on targets in Kuwait, Bahrain, and the UAE.

Türkiye is in active talks with Italy to purchase and co-produce the SAMP/T air defense system, developed by the Eurosam consortium (with MBDA and Thales Group). This system can defend against aircraft, drones, cruise missiles, and some ballistic missiles, especially with the newer SAMP/T NG version using Aster 30 missiles. Source: Bloomberg

🇺🇦 The Russian Armed Forces have struck a refinery in the Kharkov region - the company has suffered significant losses, according to Ukrainian media. The plant wants to resume operations but they are having trouble saying when they will be able to do so. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

Spain has called Israel’s continued attacks in Lebanon “unacceptable,” despite a recent U.S.–Iran two-week ceasefire. Foreign Minister José Manuel Albares said all fronts, including Lebanon, must stop.
Pope Leo welcomes the truce in Iran as a “sign of great hope.”

Even close Trump allies didn’t know what he would do. Multiple insiders believed he would reject the ceasefire offer — until he suddenly accepted it. Inside the Pentagon, officials were preparing for a massive bombing campaign. One defense official said: “We had no idea what was going to happen. It was wild.” Allies expected large-scale Iranian retaliation. Source: Axios

Just hours before Trump’s deadline to launch massive strikes, Iran’s Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei instructed negotiators to move toward a deal for the first time since the war began. An Israeli official, a regional official, and another source confirmed the shift. Two sources described Khamenei’s approval as: “A breakthrough.” A regional source added: “Without his green light, there wouldn’t have been a deal.” Due to assassination threats, Khamenei was communicating via handwritten notes carried by runners. He was personally involved in decisions Monday and Tuesday. Araghchi (Iran’s FM) pushed both the negotiations and Revolutionary Guard commanders toward accepting the deal. Source: Axios
